title: pyrethrum corymbosum subsp. achilleae
intro: Pyrethrum corymbosum subsp. achilleae, also known as the Achillea pyrethrum, is a subspecies of the Achillea corymbosa plant, a flowering perennial native to Europe and Asia, which contains the pyrethrins - a group of natural insecticides. The medicinal properties of this plant include its ability to repel insects and its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of consuming Achillea pyrethrum extracts include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, although further research is needed to confirm these effects. Herbal preparations of Achillea pyrethrum can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to treat fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.
